comment_text.sn12.60:1.1: This Nidānasutta opens in a way practically identical to the Mahānidānasutta (DN 15). However, after the introduction it merely recapitulates the teachings from the previous discourses, unlike the Mahānidānasutta which introduces a dazzling range of unique perspectives. It is hardly possible that Ānanda would make the same mistake twice, so it seems likely that the present discourse is an artificial composite.

root_text.sn12.60:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kurūsu viharati kammāsadhammaṁ nāma kurūnaṁ nigamo.
root_text.sn12.60:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā ānando yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn12.60:1.4: Yāva gambhīro cāyaṁ, bhante, paṭiccasamuppādo gambhīrāvabhāso ca, atha ca pana me uttānakuttānako viya khāyatī”ti.
root_text.sn12.60:2.2: Gambhīro cāyaṁ, ānanda, paṭiccasamuppādo gambhīrāvabhāso ca.
root_text.sn12.60:2.3: Etassa, ānanda, dhammassa ananubodhā appaṭivedhā evamayaṁ pajā tantākulakajātā kulagaṇṭhikajātā muñjapabbajabhūtā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ saṁsāraṁ nātivattati.
root_text.sn12.60:3.1: Upādāniyesu, ānanda, dhammesu assādānupassino viharato taṇhā pavaḍḍhati.
root_text.sn12.60:4.2: Tassa yāni ceva mūlāni adhogamāni, yāni ca tiriyaṅgamāni, sabbāni tāni uddhaṁ ojaṁ abhiharanti.
root_text.sn12.60:4.3: Evañhi so, ānanda, mahārukkho tadāhāro tadupādāno ciraṁ dīghamaddhānaṁ tiṭṭheyya.
root_text.sn12.60:4.4: Evameva kho, ānanda, upādāniyesu dhammesu assādānupassino viharato taṇhā pavaḍḍhati.
root_text.sn12.60:5.1: Upādāniyesu, ānanda, dhammesu ādīnavānupassino viharato taṇhā nirujjhati.
root_text.sn12.60:6.3: So taṁ rukkhaṁ mūle chindeyya, mūle chetvā palikhaṇeyya, palikhaṇitvā mūlāni uddhareyya antamaso usīranāḷimattānipi.
root_text.sn12.60:6.6: phāletvā sakalikaṁ sakalikaṁ kareyya, sakalikaṁ sakalikaṁ karitvā vātātape visoseyya, vātātape visosetvā agginā ḍaheyya, agginā ḍahetvā masiṁ kareyya, masiṁ karitvā mahāvāte vā ophuṇeyya, nadiyā vā sīghasotāya pavāheyya.
root_text.sn12.60:6.7: Evañhi so, ānanda, mahārukkho ucchinnamūlo assa tālāvatthukato anabhāvaṅkato āyatiṁ anuppādadhammo.
root_text.sn12.60:6.8: Evameva kho, ānanda, upādāniyesu dhammesu ādīnavānupassino viharato taṇhā nirujjhati.

translation_text.sn12.60:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Kurus, near the Kuru town named Kammāsadamma.
translation_text.sn12.60:1.2: Then Venerable Ānanda went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn12.60:1.4: in that this dependent origination is deep and appears deep, yet to me it seems as plain as can be.”
translation_text.sn12.60:2.2: This dependent origination is deep and appears deep.
translation_text.sn12.60:2.3: It is because of not understanding and not penetrating this teaching that this population has become tangled like string, knotted like a ball of thread, and matted like rushes and reeds, and it doesn’t escape the places of loss, the bad places, the underworld, transmigration.
translation_text.sn12.60:3.1: There are things that fuel grasping. When you concentrate on the gratification provided by these things, your craving grows.
translation_text.sn12.60:3.2: Craving is a requirement for grasping.
translation_text.sn12.60:3.3: Grasping is a requirement for continued existence.
translation_text.sn12.60:3.4: Continued existence is a requirement for rebirth.
translation_text.sn12.60:3.5: Rebirth is a requirement for old age and death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress to come to be.
translation_text.sn12.60:3.6: That is how this entire mass of suffering originates.
translation_text.sn12.60:4.1: Suppose there was a great tree.
translation_text.sn12.60:4.2: And its roots going downwards and across all draw the sap upwards.
translation_text.sn12.60:4.3: Fed and fuelled by that, the great tree would stand for a long time.
translation_text.sn12.60:4.4: In the same way, there are things that fuel grasping. When you concentrate on the gratification provided by these things, your craving grows.
translation_text.sn12.60:4.5: Craving is a requirement for grasping.
translation_text.sn12.60:4.6: Grasping is a requirement for continued existence. …
translation_text.sn12.60:4.7: That is how this entire mass of suffering originates.
translation_text.sn12.60:5.1: There are things that fuel grasping. When you concentrate on the drawbacks of these things, your craving ceases.
translation_text.sn12.60:5.3: When grasping ceases, continued existence ceases. …
translation_text.sn12.60:5.4: That is how this entire mass of suffering ceases.
translation_text.sn12.60:6.1: Suppose there was a great tree.
translation_text.sn12.60:6.2: Then a person comes along with a spade and basket.
translation_text.sn12.60:6.3: They’d cut the tree down at the roots, dig them up, and pull them out, down to the fibers and stems.
translation_text.sn12.60:6.4: Then they’d split the tree apart, cut up the parts, and chop it into splinters. They’d dry the splinters in the wind and sun, burn them with fire, and reduce them to ashes. Then they’d winnow the ashes in a strong wind, or float them away down a swift stream.
translation_text.sn12.60:6.7: In this way the great tree is cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, obliterated, and unable to arise in the future.
translation_text.sn12.60:6.8: In the same way, there are things that fuel grasping. When you concentrate on the drawbacks of these things, your craving ceases.
translation_text.sn12.60:6.10: When grasping ceases, continued existence ceases.
translation_text.sn12.60:6.11: When continued existence ceases, rebirth ceases.
translation_text.sn12.60:6.12: When rebirth ceases, old age and death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress cease.
translation_text.sn12.60:6.13: That is how this entire mass of suffering ceases.”

variant_text.sn12.60:2.3: kulagaṇṭhikajātā → guḷāguṇṭhikajātā (bj); guḷīguṇṭhikajātā (sya-all, km); guḷigandhikajātā (pts1ed, pts2ed) | muñjapabbajabhūtā → muñjababbajabhūtā (bj)

reference_text.sn12.60:1.1: ms12S2_426, msdiv60, ndp13.79, pts-vp-pli2.92, sya16.111, vri24.82
